author: lu_pinto
content:
Hi <@402853454126776320> quoting the hackmd, what I'm looking after answering is this bit:

> 3. Why not charge account creation inside of create-frame?
> 
> It introduces a semantic change silently. Currently (before 8037) the account creation is pre-charged before the create-frame and OOG the parent frame if budget is insufficient. Re-locating the account creation into the create-frame will OOG the child frame only if budget is insufficient.
> 
> This behavior change may introduce some unexpected compatibility issue for on-chain contracts.

I'm looking at the code in @main for besu and I can't seem to find where the account creation is charged in advance. From what I can see charges are only the following:
  - txCreateCost() (32000)
  - memoryExpansionGasCost - reading the initcode from memory
  - initcodeCost(inputSize) — EIP-3860 word cost
  - CREATE2 only: keccakCost(inputSize) for hashing the initcode
Is this something that was introduced in current devnet and I might not be synced with the latest? Can you show me where you are doing it in Geth?
